subject: Anthropology
title: Bilateral Descent
description: A short lesson to help you apply Bilateral Descent.

hook.text: You inherit from both parents equally. That sounds normal to Westerners, but globally it's actually unusual.

buildup.text: Bilateral descent traces kinship equally through both parents. It's common in Western and many foraging societies.

discovery.text: It creates kindreds, not lineages. Your kin group overlaps with but isn't identical to your sibling's kin group.

twist.text: Bilateral systems lack clear corporate groups. Without lineages, other institutions like law and the state fill the gap.

climax.text: Bilateral descent suits mobile, flexible societies. It trades the strength of lineages for individual choice.

punchline.text: Two lines of descent means no single group owns you.

quiz.question: What is bilateral descent?
options: Tracing kinship through the father only
Tracing kinship equally through both parents
Tracing kinship through the mother only
